frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer system title screen visible
Laparoscopic instrument visible entering the trainer box field of view
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Timer starts when first instrument becomes visible

frame_analyses.description: Instruments positioned around Penrose drain with suture visible, beginning placement through marked locations
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good visualization of the task setup with suture ready for placement

frame_analyses.description: Suture being threaded through the Penrose drain at marked locations
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise needle handling during suture placement phase

frame_analyses.description: External view showing hands loading needle with suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper extracorporeal preparation of suture before task execution

frame_analyses.description: Instruments manipulating suture through both marked positions on the drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing suture placement through both required marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning formation of first throw with suture loops visible around drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial knot formation with proper loop configuration for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Completing the surgeon's knot (double throw) as first throw with right hand manipulation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good technique forming the required double throw for first throw

frame_analyses.description: External view showing hands performing second throw with hand switching
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching between throws, left hand now dominant

frame_analyses.description: Final throw formation with continued hand switching pattern
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing the required three-throw sequence with proper hand alternation

frame_analyses.description: Cutting suture ends with scissors inside the trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper intracorporeal cutting technique to complete the task

frame_analyses.description: Thank you screen indicating task completion

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot (double throw) formation as required
Single throw with proper hand switching from throw 1
Final single throw with proper hand switching from throw 2

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit properly closed without visible gaps after knot tightening

technique_summary: Excellent performance with proper surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching pattern, and good suture placement accuracy. Task completed efficiently within good time parameters.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly faster transitions between knot throws to optimize timing
Maintain consistent suture tension throughout knot formation
